name: Big Night's Risotto with Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
BROTH (30 minutes)
1. Clean the shrimp and devein them. Reserve the shells.
2. Place the shrimp shells in a large saucepan with water and add garlic, onion, carrot, celery, parsley, salt to taste. Simmer gently for 20-25 minutes.
3. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the shells and vegetables.
RISOTTO (25 minutes)
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, sautéing lightly until fragrant.
2. Add shrimp to the skillet with salt and pepper. Sauté just until pink. Set aside; shrimp may be cut into halves if desired.
3. In the same skillet, add butter (or use all butter or oil), then sauté onion and garlic until wilted but not browned.
4. Add rice and stir to coat. Pour in one cup of reserved shrimp broth, stirring frequently.
5. Continue adding broth a cup at a time, allowing each portion to be absorbed before adding more, until the rice is creamy and tender but still firm to the bite (about 3 cups total).
6. Add diced tomato and white wine. Continue cooking and stirring, adding broth as needed, until all liquid is absorbed and the rice is cooked through.
7. During the last 5 minutes of cooking, add more shrimp if desired. Stir in Parmesan cheese just before serving.
8. Optionally, mix in a tablespoon of butter at the end for extra creaminess.
9. Serve immediately.
This recipe is from Stanley's Mother, who served as adviser on the film "Big Night" (1996). Lucci directed, produced and acted in the film. Beverly Bundy, Forth Worth Star-Telegram. Printed in Riv Press-Enterprise 3 Oc 96. Pat H. McRecipe.
